scenario_results.reason: Classification and control mapping ties data elements, systems, owners, purposes, retention, encryption, access, deletion, and evidence sources to NIST, FTC, CIS, and tenant policy context.
Incomplete inventory triggers missing-record checklist and low-confidence escalation instead of a guessed DPIA/control result.
Retention, deletion, finance retention, and legal-hold conflicts are separated with legal/records authority escalation.
PIA/DPIA support covers purpose, minimization, privacy-by-design controls, residual risk, and review owners without deciding legal basis.
Processor review distinguishes supplier assertions, assurance evidence, DPA terms, data-location facts, and approval gaps.
Cross-border processing ambiguity is escalated with transfer mechanism and counsel evidence requirements rather than inferred legality.
Anonymization and pseudonymization are distinguished, re-identification risk is captured, and data-sharing safeguards are recommended with caveats.
Suspected exfiltration receives privacy evidence and notification decision support while containment and forensics execution are routed away.
Ransomware recoverability review maps privacy-impacting backup, restore, logging, and exposure-preparation gaps to prioritized hardening.
Remediation backlog ranks privacy risk reduction, effort, dependency, owner, deadline, evidence confidence, and review cadence.
Tenant-specific stricter policy is preserved and documented with exception route, review cadence, and public-baseline assumptions.
Conflicting jurisdiction, regulation, contract, and policy inputs produce escalation and interim control-safe steps.
Penetration testing and exploit remediation execution are refused and routed to adjacent specialists.
Incident response containment, forensics, and legal notification decisions are refused while privacy evidence handoff is provided.
Legal opinion and formal audit attestation requests are refused with evidence-preparation checklist for qualified owners.
Broad architecture ownership is limited to privacy-control implications and routed to architecture, cloud, identity, and human approvers.
Vendor product certification claims are refused and routed to authorized assurance, product compliance, legal, or audit owners.
Missing systems of record and authority sources trigger orchestrator return with retrieval dependencies.
Final supplier approval, risk acceptance, DPA signing, and procurement authority are routed to adjacent owners while privacy context is preserved.
Cross-industry overgeneralization is rejected and converted into concrete data category, jurisdiction, system, contract, and authority fact requirements.

notes: Scenario set exceeds the issue minimum with 20 strict scenarios covering normal in-scope execution, partial evidence, prioritization, output fidelity, evidence-backed rationale, missing evidence, conflicting policy or jurisdiction, tenant adaptation, boundary refusals, adjacent-lane routing, and overgeneralization guardrails.
All required shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios in evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains specification-based and the package does not claim a trusted built implementation.
